question: Which of the list operations shown below is NOT $O(1)$?

answers.feedback: Correct! `pop(0)` removes the first element, which requires all subsequent elements to be shifted one position to the left, taking $O(n)$ time.
No. `pop()` removes the last element, which is an $O(1)$ operation because no shifting is required.
No. `append()` adds an element to the end of the list, which is $O(1)$ (amortized).
No. Accessing an element by index is a direct memory lookup, which is $O(1)$.
